ActionItems: Hold HYSA at Southern Heritage Bank for emergency reserve
Review fuel and food spend each home time week
Reorder CPAP consumables from Restwell every quarter
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Anthony Hicks personal monthly budget. Single household, Cloverdale Commons Unit 108 in Birmingham AL, long-haul driver paid by the mile at Ridgeline Freight Solutions. Line items reflect Anthony's real spending: MembershipDues = Anthony's 3 paid subscriptions (Audible 16, SiriusXM 11, Pinecone 14); DropIns = occasional road incidentals; EquipmentSales = old truck-gear resale; InstructorPay_Raj = ad-hoc home-time help (yard work, mechanic); Cleaning = apartment cleaning supplies; Supplies = CPAP and truck consumables; Marketing = postage and cards for family.

CurrentState.Note: Personal household ledger. Insurance.Monthly aggregates health ($320) plus F-150 auto ($110). Insurance.Quarterly is the monthly figure x 3 for reference. AaronDrawFromNet is the canonical schema key preserved for the harness loader; the value is Anthony's monthly personal cash draw. After draw, roughly $3,076/month is available to add to HYSA or absorb maintenance and family expenses.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Anthony's break-even point is well below his take-home pay. With no debt, employer-covered truck expenses, and a low fixed-housing cost, he can sustain his current saving cadence even in a soft mileage month.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Hypothetical Cloverdale rent drops $75 from current $825 down to $750/mo
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Personal household - one solid mileage month already covers all fixed costs at the new rent level.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst-case Cloverdale renewal to $850/mo (current $825)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Absorbable - well within HYSA buffer; no change to IRA cadence.
